# Conan

> Conan is a native macOS desktop app that wraps and observes Claude Code,
> giving developers a live HUD over their agent sessions — a streaming timeline
> of every prompt, tool call, and skill; live context and token usage; a
> throughput pulse; the skills and MCP servers in play; and built-in radio.

Conan runs entirely on your Mac and reads Claude Code's local session data in
`~/.claude` — no telemetry, nothing about your code or prompts leaves your
machine. It does not replace Claude Code or run the model itself; it adds the
cockpit around it, reading the activity Claude Code already writes to disk, so
the agent runs at full speed.

Pricing: one app, freemium. The free download is a genuinely useful live
observer (terminal, Context, Usage, a basic Timeline). Premium unlocks the
insight layer — skill-scoring rows, Plan/Loop/Build rows, expandable tool
payloads, longer Pulse history, Skills' last-fired times, the MCP auth
watchdog, and Claude Radio — for **$29 once**: no subscription, no per-device
limit, lifetime updates within the 1.x line. Sold via Polar.sh.

Platform: macOS 13 or later on Apple silicon. Windows and Linux are not yet
available — a waitlist on the site collects interest.

Conan is built by WhatMatters and is **not** affiliated with or endorsed by
Anthropic, nor with Conan Properties International (the "Conan the Barbarian"
franchise). "Conan" here refers to the macOS app for observing Claude Code.

## Pages

- [Home](https://conan.sh/): What Conan is, the feature HUD (Timeline, Context, Pulse, Skills & MCP, Radio), FAQ, and download
- [Terms of Service](https://conan.sh/terms): Terms governing use of Conan and conan.sh
- [Privacy Policy](https://conan.sh/privacy): What the app and site collect — and what they deliberately don't

## Optional

- [Claude Code](https://www.anthropic.com/claude-code): The Anthropic coding agent that Conan observes
